tasks.name: N3:[RX Power] QAM2, QAM4, QAM16  - rerun FE Block for outlier measurements.
tasks.description:
**Why:** Ixana wants to publish the data sheet for QS222 and hence needed to complete the RX power validation.

**Deliverable:** Get the power measurements for the FE block outlier cases from the earlier task where the power measurements are taken for QAM2, QAM4, QAM16 modes. The FE current is expected to be same across the various ADC clock frequencies. We are seeing higher current for lower ADC clock frequencies. This need to be rerun to rule out any measurement issues and confirm the reproducibility of the issue.

**Assumptions:** Hope the instruments and the data collection scripts work as expected for these outlier cases as well.

comments.text: Due dates will be assigned later.

tasks.name: N3: [RX Power] QAM16 power measurements with on chip supply - spot check
N3: full schedule implementation for QS222
tasks.description:
**What & Why**
Specify and test complex schedules through the IC testbench.

**Implementation Plan**
*   build reg\_config on python, which is loaded onto the device
    *   validate

**Completion criteria**
*   PER tests with complex schedule pass

comments.text:
When testing the below schedule the design that I had implemented for json files was not enough.

HUB: [IDLE, TX, TX+ACK, RX+ACK, RX]
NODE: [IDLE, RX, RX+ACK, TX+ACK, RX]

Updated the json file to include src and dest per descriptor.

when I ran the above schedule we ran into issue where the devices were not receiving acks because the hub uses addresses from its regs configs to send acks rather than the addresses in the packet.

Which forced us to have same address for all the nodes.
image.png

Changed the JSON file again to not take any addresses and only the devices in the network.

There was an issue in wirsdk firmware where we were calculating the no of packets sent per desc based on the tx_done flag in the irq1 status reg. This set high even after transmission of acks back to node which resulted counting acks as packets. Fixed it by incrementing packets only if we have queued packets to be sent in the previous frame.
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

comments.text:
Schedules:
 1.
     HUB: [TX, IDLE, RX, IDLE]
     NODE: [RX, IDLE, TX, IDLE]
 2.
     HUB: [TX+ACK, IDLE, RX+ACK, IDLE]
     NODE: [RX+ACK, IDLE, TX+ACK, IDLE]
3. Single and multipacket (3)
    HUB: [IDLE, TX, TX+ACK, RX+ACK, RX]
     NODE: [IDLE, RX, RX+ACK, TX+ACK, RX]

Testcases:

1. rs and crc

2. all possible combinations and transitions
     1. without rs+crc
     2. with rs+crc

3. hub tx + i, node node rx + i: 100->1800 in steps of 100. Run it for 10K times.
     1. without rs+crc
     2. with rs+crc

3. hub tx_ack + i, node node rx_ack + i: 100->1800 in steps of 100. Run it for 10K times.
     1. without rs+crc
     2. with rs+crc for tx and not ack
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

comments.text:
@Suhaib Ishaque I will be validating bidirectional communication between hub and node in baseband without ECC.

Hub Schedule: ["TX", "IDLE", "RX", "IDLE"]
Node Schdeule: ["RX", "IDLE", "TX", "IDLE"]

I will run the following iterations

hub tx and  hub rx at qam 2,4,16,64
hub tx at qam 2 hub rx at qam 4

And a few more variations of the test.

Next I will try out the same schedule with more than one transaction per slot.

Post which I will add a few more descriptors to try and make it more robust.
comments.author: Kumaresh Dhotrad

comments.text:
ADC_CLK_Scaling_QAM4_debug.pdfDebug Observation:
 We are noticing a trend where increasing dr_scale combined with a reduction in ADC clock frequency is leading to improved sensitivity.
Issue:
 This behavior contradicts the original design expectation. The intended outcome was that sensitivity should degrade under these conditions.
Next Step:
 This discrepancy requires further debugging with the help of the design and validation teams to identify the root cause. with help of @sumon da.

tasks.name: N3: [Baseband] Baseband testing of QAM, DSSS & Adiabatic in schedule mode without IDLE slots with ACK.
tasks.description:
doc:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-c9ca6436-5131-4f03-8e8f-38bf36240230)

**Why:** Need to have QS222 validated in the baseband testing in Schedule mode with ACK for a through validation of QS222.

**Deliverable:**
Conditions: SPI -24 MHz, SYSCLK= 10 MHz ; Onboard supply.
100 packets - header on. a) no encoding, b) crc, c) rs+crc, max payload size 255, 127, and max parity 32, Header RS Parity = 1
1. QAM 2- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 6 in step of 1
2. QAM 4- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 6 in step of 1
3. QAM 16- packet size -16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 6 in step of 1
4. QAM 64- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 6 in step of 1
5. DSSS QAM2 (BPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 1800 dr\_scale = 3 to 10 in step of 1
6. DSSS QAM4 (QPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 1800 dr\_scale = 3 to 10 in step of 1
7. Adiabatic QAM2 (BPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 12 in step of 1
8. Adiabatic QAM4 (QPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 12 in step of 1

  

**Risks (T,E):**

**Limitations:** (Assumptions(A), Constraints (C), Dependencies(D)) :

**Results:** Anish will provide an update here (link to the results) after validation is completed.

tasks.name: N3: [Other_Misc] version4 - Debug Temp sense test (readout of ADC code).
tasks.description:
This is an extension to the previous debug studies of temp sensor : N3: \[Misc\_test\] TEMPARATURE SENSOR TEST ([https://app.clickup.com/t/86dy17k0m](https://app.clickup.com/t/86dy17k0m))

**Why:** Ixana's QS222 - have a temp sensor that needs to be validated for the proper functioning of the QS222 product.
**Deliverable:** Measure the temperature analog value and digital code in analog and digital path
**Risks (T,E):** Risk: T- If it does not function as intended, we may need to proceed with a re-tapeout.
**Limitations: (Assumptions(A), Constraints (C), Dependency )**
C: Constraint: Since Ixana post silicon lab doesn't have means to test at the other temperatures - it is assumed that the temp sensor readings from QS222 at room temp is good enough indicator for the working of the temp sensor.
A: Assumption- Setup and config are correct

**Required documents for reference:**
Test Description:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-641deb0a-acf8-4d5d-9f5b-988569c5aee9)

tasks.name: N3: [Supplies] VDD_0p8 drops to 0 after chip_en being reset
tasks.description:
**Why:** QS222 - needs to be validated with internal supplies as a power source.

**Deliverable:**
a) Complete debug studies and provide a clear working solution for the QS222 - validation with the internal supplies (0.8 and 1.2V) or aka SIMO output.

**Risks: (T) :** If QS 222 - cannot be validated with internal supply as power source, then design and architecture needs to come up with an alternative plan and provide a fix for the QS 222 validation. Fallback option is to use the external supply.

**Limitations: (C):** Design help for debug.
**(A):** Issue observed is replicated all the time and can provide insights to the problem observed.

**Results:** Anish / Aniruddh will post the debug studies results here.

comments.text: @Pushpal Das @Sarat Anumula why this task is still open? This task was long back done.

tasks.name: N3: [TX Validation] Debugstudies of SPI read /write with internal supply
tasks.description:
**Why:** Need to complete the debug of the SPI read write with internal supply.

**Deliverable:** Document the learnings from the SPI read write debug and share it with the post silicon team.

**Risk: T:** If the SPI read write doesn't work properly with internal supply, then QS222 validation cannot be done using the internal supply and has to rely only on the external supply - not a product feature. Hence, this debug is very important to complete.

**Limitations:**
Constraints: reset\_ic() function call
Assumption: Config and set are working as expected.

**Results:** Anish will share the results here after the task complete.

comments.text:
The reason as to why the power glitches are happening  - VDD_0p8 and VDD_1p2 supplies - when supplied from onchip - is that the power supplies are not able to properly discharge when the chip_en is deasserted, making the power supplies to continue to provide the power to the QS222 - causing the chip to go into an undesired state. 

When there is a load connected to the power supplies - things seem to be working fine. (as of now - this is being used as a work around - the load can be around 100 ohm or 200 ohm).
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: SPI read write problem is root caused to the power glitches happening during the chip_en toggling.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Reason for SPI Failure:​
When I reset the IC, both supplies initially come up, but one of them (either 1P2 or 0P8) randomly begins to drop. I believe this is not a configuration issue but rather related to the power sequencing or another underlying factor.​
In the waveform shown below (Blue = 0p8, Yellow = 1p2), no SPI writes are performed—only the IC reset. After the reset, the 0p8 rail drops to zero.
 image.png


In this figure, I was performing a standard SPI read/write test and observed that both supply voltages were decaying. The random pass/fail behavior appears to be related to the supply sequence.​
The supplies should drop sufficiently for the device to return to a valid state. However, they were not always decaying quickly enough, leaving the device in an invalid condition and preventing it from powering on correctly. In some cases, one of the supplies remained high long enough to allow the read/write operation to complete before dropping. After each reset_ic, both supplies rose again, and the cycle repeated.​
image.png
​I was able to successfully perform 100K SPI writes. However, beyond that point, the test begins to fail randomly when reset_ic is enabled after each run (I tried increasing the chip_en duration that did not help). Interestingly, if reset_ic  is disabled, the test consistently passes without any issues. This behavior needs further investigation.
comments.author: Anish Saha
